differentials.name: Ependymoma 60% ◊  Most common glial tumor in adults
differentials.raw: Ependymoma 60% ◊  Most common glial tumor in adults

differentials.name: Astrocytoma 25% ◊  Most common intramedullary tumor in children
differentials.raw: Astrocytoma 25% ◊  Most common intramedullary tumor in children

context: Prevalence:   4-10% of all CNS tumors; 20% of all intraspinal tumors in adults (35% in children) √  expansion of cord √  heterogeneous signal on T2WI √  cysts + necrosis √  variable degrees of enhancement (vast majority with some enhancement) (a)  primary: Location: › cervical region: astrocytoma › thoracic region: teratoma-dermoid, astrocytoma › lumbar region: ependymoma, dermoid (b)  metastatic: eg, malignant melanoma, breast, lung √  fluid isointense to CSF √  smooth well-defined internal margins √  thinned adjacent parenchyma √  cord atrophy √  no contrast enhancement (a)  peritumoral cyst
